etymology.description: 辵(辵) ('walk/movement') + 隹(隹) (zhuī, 'short-tail bird') = a bird that can only move forward
etymology.interpretation: The character 進(進) (jìn, 'to advance') combines the idea of movement with the imagery of a bird, symbolizing forward movement, as the bird naturally progresses forward on foot.
etymology.simplification: 進(進) was simplified to 进(進) by replacing the phonetic component 隹(隹) (zhuī, 'short-tail bird') with 井(井) (jǐng, 'well') to reduce strokes.

examples.chinese: 他决定继续努力(他決定繼續努力)，以便在职业生涯中进步(以便在職業生涯中進步)。
examples.pinyin: Tā juédìng jìxù nǔlì, yǐbiàn zài zhíyè shēngyá zhōng jìnbù.
examples.english: He decided to keep working hard in order to advance in his career.

examples.chinese: 在我们进入隧道时(在我們進入隧道時)，速度变得越来越快(速度變得越來越快)。
examples.pinyin: Zài wǒmen jìnrù suìdào shí, sùdù biàn de yuè lái yuè kuài.
examples.english: As we entered the tunnel, the speed became faster and faster.

examples.chinese: 大门刚一打开(大門剛一打開)，他就立刻走了进去(他就立刻走了進去)。
examples.pinyin: Dàmén gāng yī dǎkāi, tā jiù lìkè zǒu le jìnqù.
examples.english: As soon as the door opened, he immediately walked in.
